title: Fleet Maintenance Diagnostics, Lithium Battery Fire Safety, and Rider Violation Enforcement
summary: Paul Kalabufo details four-tier maintenance system: self-diagnostic error mode (removes vehicle from app), customer phone reports, systematic weekly rotation (10 scooters/week through full fleet), and seasonal full-fleet overhaul. All vehicles include self-sealing tires and brake failure detection. Five-step safety field check ('5 S's') performed on every vehicle touch -- given battery swap and tipover retrieval frequency, checks happen multiple times per week on most vehicles. Adding baskets to all scooters for grocery shopping. Lithium battery safety: UL-certified at cell, battery, and vehicle-electrical-system levels; practices aligned with FDNY as national leader on battery fire issues. Damaged battery protocol: visual inspection on every warehouse return, fireproof barrel storage with 'cell block' powder that vitrifies to smother fire, recycling through Redwood Materials. Warehouse electrical work done to city code with local official inspections. Violations up 51% year-over-year (outpacing 34% ride increase): two warnings then monetary fines then suspension requiring rules quiz -- approximately 200 riders suspended in 2025. Double riding prohibited; new two-person tandem vehicle coming. Meeting adjourns.

processing_notes: FDNY referenced as national leader in lithium battery safety practices -- Veo aligns Syracuse operations with FDNY standards
Cell block = fire suppression material that vitrifies (turns to glass bubble) when heated to smother battery fires
Paniagua left meeting early; her question about repeat rule-breakers relayed by Nave
Head mechanic for Veo's entire northeast region is based in Syracuse -- grew from driver to regional lead
Redwood Materials = lithium battery recycling partner; AutoZone also mentioned as recycling option
Segment 6 of 6 from Public Transportation Committee Meeting, Tuesday, February 4th, 2026
Transcript corrections applied: SU -> Syracuse University
